title: HTML Link Semantics, Base URLs, Relations, and Discovery Decisions · Inference Brief: Discovery Provenance
summary: Every href becomes a page request; base, rel, hreflang, media, downloads, forms, non-navigation links, and nofollow policy are ignored. Predict authorization, URL identity, runtime admission, Promise ownership, resource bounds, accessibility impact, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the crawler ts discovery provenance increment in semantic link-discovery ledger. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice…
objectives: Retain source page, element, attribute, raw reference, resolved URL, relation tokens, scope decision, and frontier result.
Classify empty, fragment-only, mailto, tel, javascript, data, malformed, credentialed, and unsupported references without fetching.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to classify link-bearing elements, base and relation semantics, rejected references, and discovery provenance; release evidence binds source, compiler, emitted JavaScript, lockfile, package, browser revision, container, configuration, and restored state; then change one duplicate, accessibility state, axe result, resource budget, browser readiness, or manual-review condition without overclaiming. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, Cheerio, Playwright, axe-core, files, processes, packages, containers, assistive-technology review, load, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Retain source page, element, attribute, raw reference, resolved URL, relation tokens, scope decision, and frontier result.

For Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Defend the Discovery Provenance model · Theory Discovery Pr…, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: The destination URL fully explains how it was discovered.
